threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You think you can just hop on the Tube anytime? Good luck in the mornings—it's a game of survival and scheduling your life around strikes.
Caught the 59 last night; two blokes pretending to be on a game show, shouting trivia at each other. Bus driver just rolled his eyes but smiled.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I heard the call to prayer from the mosque mixing with the bass from the open-air bar down the street. Such a Berlin moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a bar where the drinks are served by drag queens and the walls are covered in retro video game art. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction to a dog on the tracks—most just nod, while the transplants panic like it's a state of emergency.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo casually hop on the bus with a top hat in hand—only in SF do you get a wedding right after a Wednesday morning meeting.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just waltz into a plaça and find a table; locals know that at 7 PM, it's all about fighting for a spot at the bar.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still marveling at the street performers on La Rambla, while the locals just walk by, unfazed.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep bringing an umbrella out, but the locals just wear raincoats and act like it’s no big deal. That’s how you know you’re new.
Last night on the 133, a couple argued over who forgot the snacks. Meanwhile, the bus driver just shook his head, clearly seen it all before.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was rooted here when I spotted the same old man watering the same patch of weeds outside my window every Sunday, like clockwork.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner, then stumble into a techno club at 3 AM with the smell of currywurst still on your clothes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by how quickly they pull out an umbrella when the fog rolls in. Tourists just stand there, confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading commuters with a ukulele on the 21 today. He had a whole crowd just waiting for the next stop. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all quirky little shops, but they often miss the quiet, cobbled streets where locals just sit and sip coffee, away from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their confusion at the 'no entry' signs on streets like Consell de Cent—seriously, it's designed to keep traffic out, not them.
